results.question: What ownership rights does the policy owner (the Trust) have?

results.question: When is the death benefit paid under this policy?
results.expected_answer: The death benefit is payable only upon the death of the second insured to die.
results.evidence: 1.2 DEATH BENEFIT TRIGGER: The death benefit is payable only upon the death of the SECOND insured to die (survivorship basis).
results.llm_answer: The death benefit is paid upon the death of the second insured, after both insured individuals have died. The payout occurs under the survivorship basis, which requires both insureds to pass away before the benefit is paid.

results.question: Can the insurer contest this policy after the second insured has died?
results.expected_answer: No. The contestability provision states the insurer may contest only within 2 years of issue while both insureds are living.
results.evidence: 7.3 CONTESTABILITY: We may contest this policy for material misrepresentation within 2 years of issue while both insureds are living.
results.llm_answer: No, the insurer cannot contest the policy after the second insured has died because the contestability period ends two years after the issue date, and the policy may only be contested while both insureds are alive.

results.llm_answer: No, the death benefit will not be paid when the first insured dies. The payout occurs only after the second insured dies.

results.question: If there is an outstanding loan at the time the second insured dies, how does it affect the death benefit paid?
results.expected_answer: The $2,500,000 death benefit is reduced by any outstanding loans, since the death benefit is paid minus any outstanding loans.
results.evidence: 2.1 Upon the death of the second insured, we will pay $2,500,000 to the designated beneficiary, minus any outstanding loans. / 5.3 Loans reduce death benefit dollar-for-dollar
results.llm_answer: Outstanding loans reduce the death benefit dollar-for-dollar. The death benefit of $2,500,000 will be reduced by the amount of any outstanding loans at the time of the second insured’s death.

results.question: Why is this policy owned by an irrevocable trust rather than by the insureds directly?
results.expected_answer: The policy is owned by an irrevocable trust to exclude the proceeds from the insureds' taxable estates, as proceeds paid to the trust should not be includable in either insured's estate if the trust is properly structured.
results.evidence: 8.1 This policy is owned by an irrevocable trust to exclude proceeds from the insureds' taxable estates. / 8.2 IMPORTANT TAX NOTE: Proceeds paid to the trust should not be includable in either insured's estate if the trust is properly structured and maintained.
results.llm_answer: The policy is owned by an irrevocable trust to exclude the death benefit proceeds from the taxable estates of the insureds, ensuring estate tax liquidity and minimizing estate tax liability.

───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
ARTICLE VII - EXCLUSIONS
───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
7.1 SUICIDE CLAUSE: If either insured commits suicide within 
    2 years of the issue date, the death benefit is limited to 
    return of premiums paid.

7.2 This suicide clause resets if the policy is reinstated.

7.3 CONTESTABILITY: We may contest this policy for material 
    misrepresentation within 2 years of issue while both insureds 
    are living.

7.4 NO OTHER EXCLUSIONS: No war, aviation, or travel exclusions 
    apply to this policy.

───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
ARTICLE VIII - ESTATE TAX PLANNING PROVISIONS
───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
8.1 This policy is owned by an irrevocable trust to exclude 
    proceeds from the insureds' taxable estates.

8.2 IMPORTANT TAX NOTE: Proceeds paid to the trust should not be 
    includable in either insured's estate if the trust is properly 
    structured and maintained.

8.3 Consult with estate planning attorney regarding:
    • Three-year lookback rule if policy transferred to trust
    • Crummey power requirements
    • Annual exclusion gift planning

───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
ARTICLE IX - SIMULTANEOUS DEATH
───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
9.1 If both insureds die in the same accident or incident and it 
    cannot be determined who died first, the death benefit will 
    be paid immediately.

9.2 If deaths occur within 30 days of each other, this will be 
    treated as simultaneous for claim purposes.

───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
ARTICLE X - OWNERSHIP AND ASSIGNMENT
───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
10.1 The policy owner (the Trust) has all ownership rights 
     including the right to:
     • Designate and change beneficiaries
     • Assign the policy
     • Take policy loans
     • Surrender the policy

10.2 Neither insured has ownership rights unless specified in 
     the trust document.

10.3 Policy may not be assigned without trustee authorization.

───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
ARTICLE XI - FREE LOOK PERIOD
───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
The policy owner may cancel within 30 days of policy receipt for 
a full refund of premium paid.

ARTICLE VII - EXCLUSIONS
───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
7.1 SUICIDE CLAUSE: If either insured commits suicide within 
    2 years of the issue date, the death benefit is limited to 
    return of premiums paid.

7.2 This suicide clause resets if the policy is reinstated.

7.3 CONTESTABILITY: We may contest this policy for material 
    misrepresentation within 2 years of issue while both insureds 
    are living.

7.4 NO OTHER EXCLUSIONS: No war, aviation, or travel exclusions 
    apply to this policy.

───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
ARTICLE VIII - ESTATE TAX PLANNING PROVISIONS
───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
8.1 This policy is owned by an irrevocable trust to exclude 
    proceeds from the insureds' taxable estates.

8.2 IMPORTANT TAX NOTE: Proceeds paid to the trust should not be 
    includable in either insured's estate if the trust is properly 
    structured and maintained.

8.3 Consult with estate planning attorney regarding:
    • Three-year lookback rule if policy transferred to trust
    • Crummey power requirements
    • Annual exclusion gift planning

───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
ARTICLE IX - SIMULTANEOUS DEATH
───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
9.1 If both insureds die in the same accident or incident and it 
    cannot be determined who died first, the death benefit will 
    be paid immediately.

9.2 If deaths occur within 30 days of each other, this will be 
    treated as simultaneous for claim purposes.

───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
ARTICLE X - OWNERSHIP AND ASSIGNMENT
───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
10.1 The policy owner (the Trust) has all ownership rights 
     including the right to:
     • Designate and change beneficiaries
     • Assign the policy
     • Take policy loans
     • Surrender the policy

10.2 Neither insured has ownership rights unless specified in 
     the trust document.

10.3 Policy may not be assigned without trustee authorization.

───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
ARTICLE XI - FREE LOOK PERIOD
───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
The policy owner may cancel within 30 days of policy receipt for 
a full refund of premium paid.

───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
ARTICLE VII - EXCLUSIONS
───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
7.1 SUICIDE CLAUSE: If either insured commits suicide within 
    2 years of the issue date, the death benefit is limited to 
    return of premiums paid.

7.2 This suicide clause resets if the policy is reinstated.

7.3 CONTESTABILITY: We may contest this policy for material 
    misrepresentation within 2 years of issue while both insureds 
    are living.

7.4 NO OTHER EXCLUSIONS: No war, aviation, or travel exclusions 
    apply to this policy.

───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
ARTICLE VIII - ESTATE TAX PLANNING PROVISIONS
───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
8.1 This policy is owned by an irrevocable trust to exclude 
    proceeds from the insureds' taxable estates.

8.2 IMPORTANT TAX NOTE: Proceeds paid to the trust should not be 
    includable in either insured's estate if the trust is properly 
    structured and maintained.

8.3 Consult with estate planning attorney regarding:
    • Three-year lookback rule if policy transferred to trust
    • Crummey power requirements
    • Annual exclusion gift planning

───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
ARTICLE IX - SIMULTANEOUS DEATH
───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
9.1 If both insureds die in the same accident or incident and it 
    cannot be determined who died first, the death benefit will 
    be paid immediately.

9.2 If deaths occur within 30 days of each other, this will be 
    treated as simultaneous for claim purposes.

───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
ARTICLE X - OWNERSHIP AND ASSIGNMENT
───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
10.1 The policy owner (the Trust) has all ownership rights 
     including the right to:
     • Designate and change beneficiaries
     • Assign the policy
     • Take policy loans
     • Surrender the policy

10.2 Neither insured has ownership rights unless specified in 
     the trust document.

10.3 Policy may not be assigned without trustee authorization.

───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
ARTICLE XI - FREE LOOK PERIOD
───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
The policy owner may cancel within 30 days of policy receipt for 
a full refund of premium paid.

id,	content
1,	documentation.

───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
ARTICLE III - FIRST DEATH BENEFITS
───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
3.1 When the first insured dies, no death benefit is paid, but:
    (a) Policy continues in force
    (b) Premium payment obligation continues
    (c) Policy owner receives written confirmation
    (d) Death certificate must be filed with us

3.2 The surviving insured becomes a single-life whole life policy 
    for administrative purposes only. Coverage amount and premium 
    remain unchanged.

───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
ARTICLE IV - PREMIUM STRUCTURE
───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
4.1 PREMIUM GUARANTEE PERIOD: Annual premium of $32,400 is 
    guaranteed level for the first 10 policy years.

4.2 AFTER YEAR 10: Premium may increase but will never exceed 
    the maximum guaranteed premium of $48,600 per year.

4.3 Premiums are payable annually on August 20th.

4.4 FIRST DEATH PREMIUM CONTINUATION: Premiums continue after 
    the first death at the same rate.

4.5 WAIVER OPTION: Premium waiver is not available on joint 
    survivorship policies.

4.6 GRACE PERIOD: If payment is not received, you have 60 days 
    to pay before the policy cancels.

───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
ARTICLE V - CASH VALUE AND LOANS
───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
5.1 This policy accumulates cash value on a guaranteed schedule.

5.2 Projected cash value (based on current assumptions):
    End of Year 10: $285,000
    End of Year 20: $712,000

5.3 POLICY LOANS:
    • Available after policy has been in force for 2 years
    • Maximum: 90% of cash surrender value
    • Interest rate: Variable, currently 5.5% per annum

ARTICLE III - FIRST DEATH BENEFITS
───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
3.1 When the first insured dies, no death benefit is paid, but:
    (a) Policy continues in force
    (b) Premium payment obligation continues
    (c) Policy owner receives written confirmation
    (d) Death certificate must be filed with us

3.2 The surviving insured becomes a single-life whole life policy 
    for administrative purposes only. Coverage amount and premium 
    remain unchanged.

───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
ARTICLE IV - PREMIUM STRUCTURE
───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
4.1 PREMIUM GUARANTEE PERIOD: Annual premium of $32,400 is 
    guaranteed level for the first 10 policy years.

4.2 AFTER YEAR 10: Premium may increase but will never exceed 
    the maximum guaranteed premium of $48,600 per year.

4.3 Premiums are payable annually on August 20th.

4.4 FIRST DEATH PREMIUM CONTINUATION: Premiums continue after 
    the first death at the same rate.

4.5 WAIVER OPTION: Premium waiver is not available on joint 
    survivorship policies.

4.6 GRACE PERIOD: If payment is not received, you have 60 days 
    to pay before the policy cancels.

───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
ARTICLE V - CASH VALUE AND LOANS
───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

ARTICLE III - FIRST DEATH BENEFITS
───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
3.1 When the first insured dies, no death benefit is paid, but:
    (a) Policy continues in force

    (c) Policy owner receives written confirmation
    (d) Death certificate must be filed with us

3.2 The surviving insured becomes a single-life whole life policy

3.1 When the first insured dies, no death benefit is paid, but:
    (a) Policy continues in force

    (c) Policy owner receives written confirmation
    (d) Death certificate must be filed with us

3.2 The surviving insured becomes a single-life whole life policy 
    for administrative purposes only. Coverage amount and premium 
    remain unchanged.

    the maximum guaranteed premium of $48,600 per year.

4.3 Premiums are payable annually on August 20th.

    the first death at the same rate.

───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
ARTICLE VII - EXCLUSIONS
───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
7.1 SUICIDE CLAUSE: If either insured commits suicide within 
    2 years of the issue date, the death benefit is limited to 
    return of premiums paid.

7.2 This suicide clause resets if the policy is reinstated.

7.3 CONTESTABILITY: We may contest this policy for material 
    misrepresentation within 2 years of issue while both insureds 
    are living.

7.4 NO OTHER EXCLUSIONS: No war, aviation, or travel exclusions 
    apply to this policy.

───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
ARTICLE VIII - ESTATE TAX PLANNING PROVISIONS
───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
8.1 This policy is owned by an irrevocable trust to exclude 
    proceeds from the insureds' taxable estates.

8.2 IMPORTANT TAX NOTE: Proceeds paid to the trust should not be 
    includable in either insured's estate if the trust is properly 
    structured and maintained.

8.3 Consult with estate planning attorney regarding:
    • Three-year lookback rule if policy transferred to trust
    • Crummey power requirements
    • Annual exclusion gift planning

───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
ARTICLE IX - SIMULTANEOUS DEATH
───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
9.1 If both insureds die in the same accident or incident and it 
    cannot be determined who died first, the death benefit will 
    be paid immediately.

9.2 If deaths occur within 30 days of each other, this will be 
    treated as simultaneous for claim purposes.

───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
ARTICLE X - OWNERSHIP AND ASSIGNMENT
───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
10.1 The policy owner (the Trust) has all ownership rights 
     including the right to:
     • Designate and change beneficiaries
     • Assign the policy
     • Take policy loans
     • Surrender the policy

6.3 This option is rarely exercised and primarily exists for

───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
ARTICLE VII - EXCLUSIONS
───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
7.1 SUICIDE CLAUSE: If either insured commits suicide within 
    2 years of the issue date, the death benefit is limited to 
    return of premiums paid.

7.2 This suicide clause resets if the policy is reinstated.

7.3 CONTESTABILITY: We may contest this policy for material 
    misrepresentation within 2 years of issue while both insureds 
    are living.

7.4 NO OTHER EXCLUSIONS: No war, aviation, or travel exclusions 
    apply to this policy.

───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
ARTICLE VIII - ESTATE TAX PLANNING PROVISIONS
───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
8.1 This policy is owned by an irrevocable trust to exclude 
    proceeds from the insureds' taxable estates.

8.2 IMPORTANT TAX NOTE: Proceeds paid to the trust should not be 
    includable in either insured's estate if the trust is properly 
    structured and maintained.

8.3 Consult with estate planning attorney regarding:
    • Three-year lookback rule if policy transferred to trust
    • Crummey power requirements
    • Annual exclusion gift planning

───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
ARTICLE IX - SIMULTANEOUS DEATH
───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
9.1 If both insureds die in the same accident or incident and it 
    cannot be determined who died first, the death benefit will 
    be paid immediately.

9.2 If deaths occur within 30 days of each other, this will be 
    treated as simultaneous for claim purposes.

───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
ARTICLE X - OWNERSHIP AND ASSIGNMENT
───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
10.1 The policy owner (the Trust) has all ownership rights 
     including the right to:
     • Designate and change beneficiaries
     • Assign the policy
     • Take policy loans
     • Surrender the policy

10.2 Neither insured has ownership rights unless specified in 
     the trust document.

10.3 Policy may not be assigned without trustee authorization.

───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
ARTICLE XI - FREE LOOK PERIOD
───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
The policy owner may cancel within 30 days of policy receipt for 
a full refund of premium paid.

    (c) Policy owner receives written confirmation
    (d) Death certificate must be filed with us

3.2 The surviving insured becomes a single-life whole life policy 
    for administrative purposes only. Coverage amount and premium 
    remain unchanged.

───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
ARTICLE IV - PREMIUM STRUCTURE
───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
4.1 PREMIUM GUARANTEE PERIOD: Annual premium of $32,400 is 
    guaranteed level for the first 10 policy years.

4.2 AFTER YEAR 10: Premium may increase but will never exceed 
    the maximum guaranteed premium of $48,600 per year.

4.3 Premiums are payable annually on August 20th.

4.4 FIRST DEATH PREMIUM CONTINUATION: Premiums continue after 
    the first death at the same rate.

4.5 WAIVER OPTION: Premium waiver is not available on joint 
    survivorship policies.

───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
ARTICLE VII - EXCLUSIONS
───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
7.1 SUICIDE CLAUSE: If either insured commits suicide within 
    2 years of the issue date, the death benefit is limited to 
    return of premiums paid.

7.2 This suicide clause resets if the policy is reinstated.

7.3 CONTESTABILITY: We may contest this policy for material 
    misrepresentation within 2 years of issue while both insureds 
    are living.

7.4 NO OTHER EXCLUSIONS: No war, aviation, or travel exclusions 
    apply to this policy.

───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
ARTICLE VIII - ESTATE TAX PLANNING PROVISIONS
───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
8.1 This policy is owned by an irrevocable trust to exclude 
    proceeds from the insureds' taxable estates.

8.2 IMPORTANT TAX NOTE: Proceeds paid to the trust should not be 
    includable in either insured's estate if the trust is properly 
    structured and maintained.

8.3 Consult with estate planning attorney regarding:
    • Three-year lookback rule if policy transferred to trust
    • Crummey power requirements
    • Annual exclusion gift planning

───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
ARTICLE IX - SIMULTANEOUS DEATH
───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
9.1 If both insureds die in the same accident or incident and it 
    cannot be determined who died first, the death benefit will 
    be paid immediately.

9.2 If deaths occur within 30 days of each other, this will be 
    treated as simultaneous for claim purposes.

───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
ARTICLE X - OWNERSHIP AND ASSIGNMENT
───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
10.1 The policy owner (the Trust) has all ownership rights 
     including the right to:
     • Designate and change beneficiaries
     • Assign the policy
     • Take policy loans
     • Surrender the policy

10.2 Neither insured has ownership rights unless specified in 
     the trust document.

10.3 Policy may not be assigned without trustee authorization.

───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
ARTICLE XI

───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
ARTICLE VII - EXCLUSIONS
───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
7.1 SUICIDE CLAUSE: If either insured commits suicide within 
    2 years of the issue date, the death benefit is limited to 
    return of premiums paid.

7.2 This suicide clause resets if the policy is reinstated.

7.3 CONTESTABILITY: We may contest this policy for material 
    misrepresentation within 2 years of issue while both insureds 
    are living.

7.4 NO OTHER EXCLUSIONS: No war, aviation, or travel exclusions 
    apply to this policy.

───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
ARTICLE VIII - ESTATE TAX PLANNING PROVISIONS
───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
8.1 This policy is owned by an irrevocable trust to exclude 
    proceeds from the insureds' taxable estates.

8.2 IMPORTANT TAX NOTE: Proceeds paid to the trust should not be 
    includable in either insured's estate if the trust is properly 
    structured and maintained.

8.3 Consult with estate planning attorney regarding:
    • Three-year lookback rule if policy transferred to trust
    • Crummey power requirements
    • Annual exclusion gift planning

───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
ARTICLE IX - SIMULTANEOUS DEATH
───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
9.1 If both insureds die in the same accident or incident and it 
    cannot be determined who died first, the death benefit will 
    be paid immediately.

9.2 If deaths occur within 30 days of each other, this will be 
    treated as simultaneous for claim purposes.

───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
ARTICLE X - OWNERSHIP AND ASSIGNMENT
───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
10.1 The policy owner (the Trust) has all ownership rights 
     including the right to:
     • Designate and change beneficiaries
     • Assign the policy
     • Take policy loans
     • Surrender the policy

10.2 Neither insured has ownership rights unless specified in 
     the trust document.

10.3 Policy may not be assigned without trustee authorization.

───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
ARTICLE XI - FREE LOOK PERIOD
───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
The policy owner may cancel within 30 days of policy receipt for 
a full refund of premium paid.
